message:
fix(storage): skip full schema rebuild on runtime-compatible legacy databases

When opening an existing database whose user_version is behind
CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION, beads_rust previously always ran the full
apply_schema() path — which includes CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, all
migrations, and index rebuilds. On databases backed by frankensqlite
this could trigger OOM failures during DROP INDEX on large files,
and the runtime parser cannot reliably reconstruct partial-index
predicates anyway.

This change adds a fast runtime_schema_compatible() check that
verifies all required tables and columns exist without running any
DDL. When the check passes, only apply_runtime_pragmas() is called
(WAL mode, synchronous, cache size) — skipping the expensive schema
and migration path entirely.

Key changes:
- Extract apply_runtime_pragmas() from apply_schema() so WAL/cache
  PRAGMAs can be applied independently of DDL
- Add runtime_schema_compatible() that checks table existence and
  column presence for all 11 required tables using PRAGMA table_info
  instead of pragma_table_info() (which frankensqlite's parser cannot
  reconstruct as a virtual table)
- Add table_has_columns() helper for multi-column existence checks
- Replace pragma_table_info() function calls with PRAGMA statements
  throughout column_exists(), kv_table_uses_primary_key(), and
  run_migrations() for consistent frankensqlite compatibility
- Remove unconditional DROP INDEX idx_issues_ready from
  run_pre_schema_migrations (causes OOM on large frankensqlite DBs)
- Open path: when user_version < CURRENT and schema is compatible,
  apply only runtime pragmas; otherwise fall through to full schema
- Add test verifying legacy KV tables with PRIMARY KEY constraints
  (vs UNIQUE index) are accepted as runtime-compatible
- Add test verifying open() skips full schema on compatible legacy DBs
- Update Cargo.lock for local frankensqlite path dependencies (new
  fsqlite-ext-icu, fsqlite-ext-misc crates; hashbrown additions)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
